channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump to meet with Senate Republican as frustrations rise within the party
item.description: Divides over what GOP priorities should be ahead of this fall's midterm elections are testing the relationship between President Trump and Senate Majority Leader John Thune.
item.content:encoded: Divides over what GOP priorities should be ahead of this fall's midterm elections are testing the relationship between President Trump and Senate Majority Leader John Thune.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Political consultant on whether Trump is losing GOP support after war powers vote
item.description: NPR's A Martínez asks Republican political consultant Doug Heye about whether Trump is losing support among congressional Republicans after the Senate voted to limit his war powers on Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martínez asks Republican political consultant Doug Heye about whether Trump is losing support among congressional Republicans after the Senate voted to limit his war powers on Iran.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Trump touts past economic achievements, instead of future plans, in speech
item.description: President Trump visited a Mack Trucks plant in Pennsylvania Tuesday, where his speech focused on past economic accomplishments, instead of future plans.
item.content:encoded: President Trump visited a Mack Trucks plant in Pennsylvania Tuesday, where his speech focused on past economic accomplishments, instead of future plans.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Why are crisis pregnancy centers saying they can 'rule out' ectopic pregnancy?
item.description: Under President Trump, more federal attention and support has gone towards anti-abortion Christian centers. A watchdog group says many of them mislead patients with promises to "rule out" ectopic pregnancies.
item.content:encoded:
Under President Trump, more federal attention and support has gone towards anti-abortion Christian centers. A watchdog group says many of them mislead patients with promises to "rule out" ectopic pregnancies.

(Image credit: Taylor Glascock for The Washington Post)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump to meet with Senate Republicans Wednesday, Congress passes largest housing affordability bill in decades, Democrats face questions about the party's future after New York primary results.
item.content:encoded: Trump to meet with Senate Republicans Wednesday, Congress passes largest housing affordability bill in decades, Democrats face questions about the party's future after New York primary results.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Does Motown's legacy endure for Detroit's younger generations?
item.description: Motown --- the music and the label -- is central to the identity of Detroiters. The music is woven together with the city's legacy. But do younger generations feel the same way?
item.content:encoded: Motown --- the music and the label -- is central to the identity of Detroiters. The music is woven together with the city's legacy. But do younger generations feel the same way?
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: Soccer stars Ronaldo and Messi set new records at World Cup
item.description: Soccer superstars Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have set new World Cup records.
item.content:encoded: Soccer superstars Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have set new World Cup records.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: With at least 6 wildfires burning, Utah officials prep for long fire season
item.description: Several wildfires have broken out in the Beehive state. One required an entire town to be evacuated for nearly a week now.
item.content:encoded: Several wildfires have broken out in the Beehive state. One required an entire town to be evacuated for nearly a week now.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Ballard

item.title: How climate change is influencing Europe's record-breaking heat wave
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Jennifer Francis, senior scientist at the Massachusetts-based Woodwell Climate Research Center, about the impact of Europe's heat wave and its links to climate change.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Jennifer Francis, senior scientist at the Massachusetts-based Woodwell Climate Research Center, about the impact of Europe's heat wave and its links to climate change.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: As U.S. government looks to leverage food aid, producers seek other delivery methods
item.description: The U.S. is changing the way food aid is distributed to starving children around the world. It is now using aid as a diplomatic tool as nutrition bars pile up in U.S. factories.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. is changing the way food aid is distributed to starving children around the world. It is now using aid as a diplomatic tool as nutrition bars pile up in U.S. factories.
item.dc:creator: Grant Blankenship
